The Gray Lady's agents watch.","tags":["region","mistlands","fog","bells"]},{"id":145,"title":"Silkstrand","category":"regions","body":"Silkstrand is a city of bridges and canals. Power flows through silk, silver, and shadow. The Matron rules, but the Ragman, Tide Father, and Silk Mother hold the underworld. Favours are measured in threads, and the ninth bridge leads to the Sunken Quarter.","tags":["region","silkstrand","city","trade"]},{"id":146,"title":"Thepyrgos","category":"regions","body":"Thepyrgos is the City of a Thousand Stairs, a coastal academic city. The Synod licenses magic; the Chain-Lanterns hunt untutored talent. The Unfinished Stair appears to those who ask forbidden questions. The Witness and the Ninth war in forgotten archives.","tags":["region","thepyrgos","stairs","synod"]},{"id":147,"title":"Valewood","category":"regions","body":"The Valewood is an ancient forest of fading empires, fae courts, and beast-kin. Lethai-al and Lethai-thora guard star-roads and sealed vats. The Hazel Queen and Alder King rule the courts. The Ninth Leaf falls to hidden thresholds.","tags":["region","valewood","forest","fae"]},{"id":148,"title":"Ykrul (Orcs)","category":"regions","body":"Ykrul are steppe nomads of the Violet Steppe. Honour measured in oaths and remounts. They ride between wolf standards. Kon'reh is their game of encirclement. They count in eights, fearing the ninth. The Khagan's Court rules from the saddle.","tags":["region","orcs","ykrul","steppe"]},{"id":149,"title":"Armor Conversion","category":"rules","body":"Armor converts Harm to Fatigue. Light: Harm 1 → Fatigue 1, Harm 2 → Harm 1 + Fatigue 1, Harm 3 → Harm 2 + Fatigue 1. Medium: Harm 1 → Fatigue 1, Harm 2 → Fatigue 1, Harm 3 → Harm 2 + Fatigue 1. Heavy: Harm 1 → Fatigue 1, Harm 2 → Fatigue 1, Harm 3 → Fatigue 2. Minimum 1 Fatigue per hit.","tags":["armor","conversion","srd"]},{"id":150,"title":"Boons","category":"rules","body":"Boons are narrative tokens. Earn 1 on Partial, 2 on Miss. Max 5, trim to 2 at scene end. Spend 1 to: re-roll a die, improve Position, activate an Asset, or convert 2 Boons to 1 XP (once per session, max 2 XP).","tags":["boons","resource","srd"]},{"id":151,"title":"Core Resolution","category":"rules","body":"Every important action follows the loop: declare action and approach, GM sets Position and DV, roll dice pool (Attribute + Skill d10s), count successes (6+ = 1 success, 10 = 2 successes), count Story Beats (1s = SB for GM), compare to DV, apply outcome from the Outcome Matrix.","tags":["resolution","core","srd"]},{"id":152,"title":"Difficulty Value (DV)","category":"rules","body":"DV determines target successes needed. 2 = routine, 3 = default, 4 = hard, 5+ = extreme. Default DV is 3.","tags":["dv","difficulty","srd"]},{"id":153,"title":"Fatigue and Harm","category":"rules","body":"Fatigue track size = Body. Each point worsens Position; if already Desperate, -1 die per Fatigue. Overflow fills track → increase Harm by 1, clear Fatigue. Harm levels: 1 (–1 die), 2 (–1 die most actions), 3 (incapacitated). Taking Harm clears existing Fatigue first.","tags":["fatigue","harm","health","srd"]},{"id":154,"title":"Outcome Matrix","category":"rules","body":"Successes ≥ DV, SB = 0 → Clean Success. Successes ≥ DV, SB > 0 → Success with SB (GM spends SB for complication). 0 < Successes < DV → Partial (gain 1 Boon). Successes = 0 → Miss (gain 2 Boons, GM escalates).","tags":["outcome","matrix","srd"]},{"id":155,"title":"Position","category":"rules","body":"Position reflects risk: Dominant (re-roll one failure), Controlled (no re-rolls), Desperate (re-roll one success). 10s are never re-rolled. Spending 1 Boon improves Position by one step.","tags":["position","risk","srd"]},{"id":156,"title":"Story Beats (SB)","category":"rules","body":"Each die showing a 1 gives the GM 1 Story Beat. Spend SB to introduce complications: 1 SB = minor (noise, tick timer), 2 SB = moderate (alarm, lesser foe), 3 SB = serious (reinforcements, gear breaks), 4+ SB = major (trap springs, scene shift).","tags":["story beats","sb","gm","srd"]},{"id":157,"title":"Always Another Exit","category":"talents","body":"Once per session, declare a prepared escape route during a heist or when cornered. The GM may impose a cost, but you get out.","tags":["talent","rogue","stealth"],"cost":4},{"id":158,"title":"Arcane Dominance","category":"talents","body":"When contesting another magical effect, automatically overpower weaker effects (Tier II or lower). At 4 segments: Navigation rolls in Acasia gain +1 DV (roads shift). At 8 segments: the Curse Stirrs complication triggers once per session (roll on the Curse Stirrs table). On failure, you gain the Condition Debtor to the Dead (any spirit can demand one service from you). On a critical failure, the Curse Awakening timer ticks +2 immediately. It is a threshold of death and memory, where the Empire's last toll is still collected.","tags":["acasia","location","road","curse"]},{"id":236,"title":"Free Companies (Acasia)","category":"factions","body":"Mercenary bands that are the only law between the Margravine and the Lame King. They trade loyalty like grain, swapping colors mid-march. Their goal: find a permanent contract before winter. Their method: selling loyalty, swapping sides for better pay. Why players care: They are the only armed force that can be hired, but they are unreliable – they may flip mid-battle, and breaking a contract with them ticks the Curse.","tags":["acasia","faction","mercenary"]},{"id":237,"title":"Hedge-witches (Acasia)","category":"lore","body":"Practitioners of threshold magic who tie red thread around bridges and leave offerings of salt at crossroads to keep the Curse from spreading. They know which bridges eat travelers. Their price is a story – one that ends badly. They are the living memory of Acasia's old ways, and they trade in debts, memories, and omens. The Margravine tolerates them; the Lame King ignores them; the Curse listens when they speak.","tags":["acasia","witchcraft","hedge","curse"]},{"id":238,"title":"The Ninth (Acasia)","category":"lore","body":"In Acasia, the ninth toll paid at a bridge is never the last. The wise pay eight and barter for the ninth. The ninth toll is collected by a child with no name – the Curse's enforcer. To count to nine, speak the ninth name, or pour the ninth cup is to invite the Hollow's attention. The Ninth Taboo is a universal warning across cultures, but in Acasia it is a living law enforced by the land itself.","tags":["acasia","ninth","taboo","curse"]}]

summary:
**Campaign Summary:**
You are in an unnamed city, hired by **Sera Vex** (scarred woman seeking answers about her missing friend **Malachai**, a Cantor) to retrieve a cask from the old aqueducts. You retrieved it, but a mysterious note—appearing in your own handwriting—claimed the cask is a decoy and the real prize lies in an **old chapel beneath the North Gate**, sealed as an "echo chamber." The note warned Sera "doesn't leave loose ends." You've learned you're the third person asking about the cask this month. With **Tomas**, a drain worker, you've entered the aqueducts through a middle-level grate. Key unresolved threads: Who sent the note and why? Is Sera trustworthy? What is in the echo chamber? What happened to Malachai? Who else seeks the prize? You now move quietly through the tunnels, Tomas guiding you deeper.

conversation.content:
*You slide into the seat across from Rik, keeping your voice low.*

"She confirmed the cask is a decoy. The real prize is under the North Gate chapel." *You pause, letting him absorb that.* "She also told me the mark on that note? It belonged to a man named Garrick. Used to run jobs out of this very tavern. Disappeared in the aqueducts three years ago. They found his body in the sump. Cask was gone."

*Rik's eyes narrow. He doesn't speak for a long moment, just turns his tankard slowly in his hands.*

"Garrick." *He says the name like it tastes bad.* "I remember him. Kept to himself. Paid in silver. Had a habit of finding things that were supposed to stay lost."

*He looks up at you.*

"If that's his mark on the note, then either someone dug up his old records, or..." *He trails off, a flicker of unease crossing his face.*

"Or what?"

*Rik meets your eyes, his voice dropping to barely a whisper.*

"Or he's not as dead as we thought."

*You let the silence hang for a moment, then nod once.*

"Good enough for tonight."

*You turn and head for the stairs, feeling Rik's eyes on your back. The stairs groan under your weight as you climb. At the top, you find the second door on the left—the one Hester mentioned.*

*You pause outside, listening. Faint movement inside. A floorboard creaks.*

*Before you can knock, the door swings open. Sera stands there, her scarred face half-lit by a candle on the bedside table. She's changed into a dry shirt, her hair still damp. She looks at you with an expression that's hard to read—part wariness, part something almost like respect.*

"Figured you'd find your way up." *She steps aside, gesturing you in.* "Close the door behind you."

*The room is small but clean. A bed, a washstand, a window overlooking the street. The cask sits on the floor in the corner, still sealed. Sera moves to the window, checking the street below before turning back.*

"So. We're alone now. No tunnels, no notes, no barkeep listening." *She crosses her arms.* "You've seen the cask. You've seen the note. You've heard what I had to say." *Her eyes meet yours, steady and unblinking.* "Now ask what you really came to ask."

*Sera takes a long breath, then meets your gaze again.*

"Malachai was a Cantor. A good one. He sang in the old chapels before the Synod locked them down. He was the one who told me about the echo chamber—said it was a silencing vault, built to contain something that shouldn't be heard."

*She taps the cask.*

"He was also the one who marked this cask with his spiral. His calling card. He used it on everything he touched."

*She looks down at the cask, her voice growing quieter.*

"He went into the aqueducts three years ago. Said he had a lead on something that would let him sing the truth into the world. Something the Synod had buried."

*She looks up at you.*

"He never came back. And now his cask surfaces with a note warning me off. Whoever wrote that note knows what happened to Malachai. And they're scared of what we might find."

*She holds your gaze, her eyes glistening.*

"I'm not here for silver. I'm here for answers. And I think that chapel under the North Gate is where they're buried."

*You hold the worn silver coin, turning it over in your fingers. The symbol is unfamiliar, but the weight is right—good silver.*

*The note crinkles in your pocket. The barkeep's words echo: "She doesn't leave loose ends." And now Sera is walking away, leaving you with a coin, a meeting time, and a warning.*

*Through the grimy window, you can see the street outside the Rusty Tankard. A figure in a dark coat lingers near a lamppost, half-hidden in shadow. Watching. Waiting. You can't tell if it's one of Sera's people, someone from the note-writer, or just a late-night loiterer.*

*The fire pops in the hearth. Hester the barkeep is watching you from behind the bar, polishing the same cup she's been working on for the last five minutes.*

*The night stretches ahead, full of choices and dangers.*

*The scarred woman's eyes flick to the pouch, then back to you. A slow, knowing smile spreads across her face.*

"Smart. Very smart."

*She takes the pouch, weighing it in her hand, then nods.*

"Here's what I know. The old aqueducts have three levels. The top level is dry—been abandoned for decades. The middle level still carries water to the eastern quarter. The bottom level..." *She pauses.* "The bottom level was sealed off after the Collapse. Official records say it was flooded and unstable. Unofficial records say something was walled in down there."

*She leans forward.*

"The cask you're looking for was last seen on the middle level, in a cistern chamber beneath the old market. But the people who took it down there didn't come back up. Three men, all experienced tunnel rats. Vanished."

*She taps the counter.*

"Your best bet is to enter through the drain grate in the old slaughterhouse district. It's the least watched, and the tunnel from there leads directly to the middle level. From the cistern chamber, you can access the lower level—if you're brave enough to go that deep."

*She slides a folded piece of paper across the bar. It's a rough map, drawn in charcoal.*

"Take it. It's not complete, but it'll keep you from getting lost in the wrong places."

*You hold Sera's gaze, keeping your voice steady.*
